name_romaji: Miyama Kannon-dō Honzon Jūichimen Kanzeon Bosatsu
name_en: Eleven-Headed Kannon, principal image of Miyama Kannon Hall
description_en: A Buddhist sculpture of the Eleven-Headed Kannon (Jūichimen Kanzeon Bosatsu), the principal image of the Miyama Kannon Hall. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Chikusei City on 1976-04-01. Located in the Miyama area.

name_en: Stone Buddha of the former Kanki-in temple, Gekkaizan
description_en: A stone Buddhist image (sekibutsu) associated with the defunct Kanki-in temple of Gekkaizan. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Chikusei City on 1976-06-28. Located in the Okazeri area.
A wooden seated sculpture of Amida Nyorai (Amitābha Buddha). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Chikusei City on 1976-06-28. Located in the Nakadate area.
A bronze standing sculpture of Shō Kanzeon Bosatsu (Āryāvalokiteśvara). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Chikusei City on 1977-03-24. Located in the Kō area.
A wooden standing sculpture of Yakushi Nyorai (the Medicine Buddha). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Chikusei City on 1979-03-01. Located in the Yokozuka area.
A standing sculpture of Shō Kanzeon Bosatsu (Āryāvalokiteśvara). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Chikusei City on 1979-03-01. Located in the Oguri area.

name_en: Buddhist statue of Kōfuku-ji temple, Kido
description_en: A Buddhist statue held at Kōfuku-ji temple in Kido. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Chikusei City on 1979-04-01. Located in the Kido area.
A bronze standing sculpture of Kanzeon Bosatsu (Avalokiteśvara). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Chikusei City on 1980-03-28.
A wooden seated sculpture of Amida Nyorai (Amitābha Buddha). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Chikusei City on 1984-03-19. Located in the Kurogo area.
A wooden seated sculpture of Yakushi Nyorai (the Medicine Buddha). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Chikusei City on 1985-05-17. Located in the Oguri area.
A wooden standing sculpture of Kishimojin (Hārītī). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Chikusei City. Located in the Chigyō area.

A wooden standing sculpture of the Eleven-Headed Kannon (Jūichimen Kannon). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Chikusei City on 1991-03-28. Located in the Goshomiya area.
A wooden standing sculpture of a deva-form guardian figure (Tenbu). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Chikusei City on 1991-03-28. Located in the Goshomiya area.

A wooden seated sculpture of Yakushi Nyorai (the Medicine Buddha). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Chikusei City on 2002-03-20. Located in the Shimohiratsuka area.

name_romaji: Mokuzō Fudō Myōō oyobi Nidōji Ryūzō
name_en: Wooden standing Fudō Myōō (Acala) and two attendant boys
description_en: A wooden standing sculpture group of Fudō Myōō (Acala) accompanied by two attendant boys (dōji). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Chikusei City on 2003-08-21. Located in the Okazeri area.
A wooden seated sculpture of Amida Nyorai (Amitābha Buddha). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Chikusei City on 2005-03-15. Located in the Sekimotokami area.
A wooden standing sculpture of Fudō Myōō (Acala). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Chikusei City on 2005-03-15. Located in the Sekimotoshimo area.
A wooden standing sculpture of Shō Kannon. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Chikusei City on 2011-08-18. Located in the Oguri area.

A wooden standing sculpture of the Eleven-Headed Kannon (Jūichimen Kannon). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Chikusei City on 2011-08-18. Located in the Oguri area.

name_en: Wooden standing Bishamonten (Vaiśravaṇa), with dedicatory sutra enshrined inside
description_en: A wooden standing sculpture of Bishamonten (Vaiśravaṇa), accompanied by a dedicatory sutra enshrined within the figure. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Chikusei City on 2023-09-22. Located in the Nakadate area.
A wooden standing sculpture of Fudō Myōō (Acala). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Chikusei City on 2023-09-22. Located in the Nakadate area.
A framed tablet (gaku) inscribed with the name Nihō-an. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (craft) by Chikusei City on 1976-06-28.

name_en: Dō-maru armor laced with dark-blue cords over solid small scales
description_en: A dō-maru cuirass constructed of solid small scales and laced with dark-blue cords (honkozane kon'ito odoshi). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (craft) by Chikusei City on 1976-06-28.
A jinbaori, a surcoat worn over armor. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (craft) by Chikusei City on 1976-06-28.
A jingasa, a lightweight war hat. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (craft) by Chikusei City on 1976-06-28.

name_romaji: Shimodate Hanshu Ishikawa-ke Hairyō Uchigatana Koshirae
name_en: Sword mounting bestowed on the Ishikawa family, lords of Shimodate domain
description_en: An uchigatana sword mounting (koshirae) bestowed upon the Ishikawa family, lords of the Shimodate domain. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (craft) by Chikusei City on 1977-03-24.

name_en: Silver hand mirror bearing a family crest
description_en: A silver hand mirror bearing a family crest. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (craft) by Chikusei City on 1977-03-24. Located in the Kō area.
A bronze five-pronged ritual vajra (gokosho). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (craft) by Chikusei City on 1983-04-15. Located in the Kurogo area.
A bronze five-pronged vajra bell (gokorei). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (craft) by Chikusei City on 1983-04-15. Located in the Kurogo area.

name_en: Maki-e inkstone box with a thatched-hut landscape design
description_en: A lacquered inkstone box (suzuribako) decorated in maki-e with a landscape featuring a thatched hut. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (craft) by Chikusei City on 1984-03-19. Located in the Kurogo area.

name_en: Miniature shrine of the Fudō Hall, former Iō-ji temple
description_en: A zushi (miniature shrine) from the Fudō Hall of the defunct Iō-ji temple (Kanki-in, Gekkaizan). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (craft) by Chikusei City on 2003-08-21. Located in the Okazeri area.
A saitanchō, an album of New Year's poems, associated with Utsunomiya. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (calligraphy) by Chikusei City on 1976-06-28.

name_romaji: Date Sakon-no-chūjō Yoshimura-kō Hitsu Jiku Ittsui
name_en: Pair of hanging scrolls brushed by Lord Date Yoshimura
description_en: A pair of hanging scrolls brushed by Date Yoshimura (Sakon-no-chūjō). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (calligraphy) by Chikusei City on 1976-06-28. Located in the Nakadate area.

name_en: Waka poem brushed by Lord Yoshimura
description_en: A waka poem brushed by Lord Yoshimura. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (calligraphy) by Chikusei City on 1976-06-28. Located in the Nakadate area.
A fossil of a whale. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material) by Chikusei City on 1976-04-01. Housed at the Akeno Community Center.
An itabi, a flat stone stupa tablet. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material) by Chikusei City on 1983-04-22. Located in the Oguri area.
An itabi, a flat stone stupa tablet. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material) by Chikusei City. Located in the Sekimotonaka area.
A gorintō, a five-ringed stone memorial pagoda. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material) by Chikusei City. Located in the Sekimotonaka area.
A spouted earthenware vessel (chūkō doki). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material) by Chikusei City on 1995-02-23.
A pedestaled earthenware vessel of the takatsuki type. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material) by Chikusei City on 1995-02-23. Located in the Nodono area.
